short: A 16-bit floating-point format that cuts memory and speeds up training and inference.
definition: FP16, or half-precision floating point, is a 16-bit number format using one sign bit, five exponent bits, and ten mantissa bits. Compared with 32-bit FP32, it halves memory use and raises throughput on GPU tensor cores, so it is widely used in mixed-precision training and inference. Because its dynamic range is narrow, training often keeps a master copy of weights in FP32; BF16 (a related 16-bit format) has since become more common for large-model training thanks to better numeric stability.
